resume.title: Software Engineer
resume.headline: Software Engineer with 5+ years in cloud infrastructure, IoT platforms, and distributed systems.
resume.summary: Software Engineer with experience building and operating globally distributed cloud services, infrastructure tooling, and customer-facing IoT platforms at Microsoft. Strong background in TypeScript, C#, .NET, Go, Kubernetes, Azure, CI/CD, reliability, and cross-team collaboration.

work_experience.title: Software Engineer II
bullets:
Owned Infrastructure for a globally distributed service of 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices (TypeScript/.NET/Go)
running on AKS (Kubernetes) clusters – utilized Docker, Helm, Istio, and related technologies in the space.

Heavily involved in PROD incident response across all service team areas, offered solutions for hot-fixing and
minimizing customer impact and service downtime.

Developed tooling and documentation to democratize Infrastructure operations to engineers across all service teams,
eliminating dev cycle bottlenecks.
Continually maintained service compliance with standards such as GDPR and FedRAMP.
Overhauled CI/CD pipelines, enabled targeted microservice deployment, tripled deployment frequency.
Implemented ‘region agnostic’ environment provisioning, reducing the time to service presence in a new region from weeks to hours.

Saved hundreds of thousands in dev cost through solutions such as ’self-serve’ dev environment provisioning in ‘leased’
subscriptions - effectively eliminating cost of unused resources.
Mentored junior engineers and fostered an environment to comfortably onboard, ask questions, and grow.

work_experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets:
Full-Stack engineer for a service that provisioned packaged ‘IoT Solutions’ and resources in the customer’s Azure
cloud subscription.

Spearheaded a UX/UI refresh with a focus on Accessibility during a time crunch after the existing Front-End engineers
had moved to a different team.

Drastically improved service reliability and customer experience by identifying and eliminating the most common
failure scenarios.

Adopted a RESTful based deployment engine to replace code that had been tightly coupled to the service’s UI, greatly
improving code maintainability.
Developed a custom testing application in Python to continually validate our E2E deployment scenario.

Delivered weekly incident reports to IoT leadership on behalf of multiple teams based in Washington and Germany,
and one third-party offering from Intel.
Gracefully deprecated the service over a 6-month period while working on a new team.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

repos.name: tailord
repos.readme_summary: Tailord generates structured, role-specific "Tailorings" that explain why a candidate is a strong fit for a specific job description. It uses a multi-stage LLM pipeline to scrape job postings, extract requirements, score candidate experience, generate an advocacy letter, and stream progress to the client as each stage completes.

experience.raw_resume_text:
Charles Dunbar
dunbarchara@gmail.com • linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar

Experience

Microsoft – Software Engineer II
 Azure IoT Central

 Redmond, WA

01/2020 - 04/2023

• Owned Infrastructure for a globally distributed service of 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices (TypeScript/.NET/Go)

running on AKS (Kubernetes) clusters – utilized Docker, Helm, Istio, and related technologies in the space.

• Heavily involved in PROD incident response across all service team areas, offered solutions for hot-fixing and

minimizing customer impact and service downtime.

• Developed tooling and documentation to democratize Infrastructure operations to engineers across all service teams,

eliminating dev cycle bottlenecks.

• Continually maintained service compliance with standards such as GDPR and FedRAMP.
• Overhauled CI/CD pipelines, enabled targeted microservice deployment, tripled deployment frequency.
• Implemented ‘region agnostic’ environment provisioning, reducing the time to service presence in a new region from weeks to hours.

• Saved hundreds of thousands in dev cost through solutions such as ’self-serve’ dev environment provisioning in ‘leased’

subscriptions - effectively eliminating cost of unused resources.

• Mentored junior engineers and fostered an environment to comfortably onboard, ask questions, and grow.

 Azure IoT Solutions

03/2018 - 12/2019

• Full-Stack engineer for a service that provisioned packaged ‘IoT Solutions’ and resources in the customer’s Azure

cloud subscription.

• Spearheaded a UX/UI refresh with a focus on Accessibility during a time crunch after the existing Front-End engineers

had moved to a different team.

• Drastically improved service reliability and customer experience by identifying and eliminating the most common

failure scenarios.

• Adopted a RESTful based deployment engine to replace code that had been tightly coupled to the service’s UI, greatly

improving code maintainability.

• Developed a custom testing application in Python to continually validate our E2E deployment scenario.
• Delivered weekly incident reports to IoT leadership on behalf of multiple teams based in Washington and Germany,

and one third-party offering from Intel.

• Gracefully deprecated the service over a 6-month period while working on a new team.

Java • TypeScript • C# • Node.js • Azure Cloud • Kubernetes • Docker • Helm • DevOps • CI/CD

Skills

Education

University of Arizona – Tucson, AZ

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

GPA • 3.8 (Magna Cum Laude)
Graduation • December 2017

Hobbies

Fitness • Sports • Arduino • 3D Modeling/Printing • Videography • Art • DIY

ad408020-c23d-4626-95bd-9e7bc6c81741.generated_output:
Hello **Linear**,

Given the requirements in your [Software Engineer](/mock/job-software-engineer-linear) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**Full-stack TypeScript work across product and platform**

Charles brings direct full-stack experience from Microsoft, where he worked on customer-facing IoT software and a deployment engine that had previously been tightly coupled to the service UI. His profile also shows TypeScript on both the backend and frontend side, plus React in his GitHub stack, which makes him a credible match for a role spanning Linear’s TypeScript backend and React frontend. The combination of RESTful API work, hands-on UI contribution, and product-facing engineering gives him a practical foundation for shipping across the stack. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**Proven ownership of reliability and scalable service operations**

At Azure IoT Central, Charles owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices, and he overhauled CI/CD to enable targeted microservice deployment and triple deployment frequency. He also worked heavily in production incident response, focused on hot-fixing issues and minimizing customer impact, while at Azure IoT Solutions he identified and eliminated the most common failure scenarios to improve reliability and customer experience. That background maps well to a team that cares deeply about performance, reliability, and scaling the product without sacrificing quality. *[Resume]*

**API and database experience that supports a developer-facing product**

Charles has direct RESTful API experience from Microsoft, including replacing UI-coupled deployment logic with a RESTful deployment engine. His GitHub project adds concrete evidence of modern backend implementation with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and PostgreSQL, which strengthens the case that he can work comfortably in API-heavy systems with relational data. While the profile does not show public GraphQL specifically, it does show the underlying backend fluency that would transfer well to a developer platform surface. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**Engineering judgment shaped by operating complex systems**

Charles’s work shows more than feature delivery: he built tooling and documentation to remove infrastructure bottlenecks, implemented region-agnostic provisioning to cut new-region setup from weeks to hours, and saved substantial dev cost through self-serve environment provisioning. He also mentored junior engineers and collaborated across teams, which suggests he can contribute thoughtfully as the product and codebase evolve. That mix of operational ownership and cross-team communication is a strong fit for a team that values fast shipping with careful technical decisions. *[Resume]*

---

Charles looks like a strong conversation candidate for Linear because his background combines full-stack TypeScript work, service reliability ownership, and practical API/backend experience. He has already operated in environments where product quality, deployment speed, and developer experience all mattered at once, which is a useful signal for this role.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.question_for_candidate: Linear’s public GraphQL API is a core part of this Software Engineer role — have you designed or implemented a GraphQL API before, and if so, could you share one concrete example of the schema, resolvers, and any performance or pagination decisions you made?
gaps.context: This matters for Linear because the role includes contributing to the product’s public GraphQL API and developer platform, so we need evidence you can design and ship GraphQL endpoints, not just REST APIs.

gaps.question_for_candidate: Linear is a desktop-first product, so have you built or shipped any Electron or other desktop app features where you owned the app shell, native integrations, or update/release flow?
gaps.context: This matters for Linear because the Software Engineer role spans polished product work across the app experience, and desktop application experience would be directly relevant to shipping and maintaining a desktop-first interface.

partials.job_requirement: Linear is building the next generation of project management software. We care deeply about product quality, performance, and developer experience. We're looking for a Software Engineer who sweats the details, ships fast, and wants to work on a product used by tens of thousands of engineering teams worldwide.
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you built or improved a product-facing feature or developer API that had to meet very high quality/performance standards for a large external user base, and what impact it had?
partials.context: For Linear, the missing evidence is a concrete example of shipping polished product or API work for a widely used developer-facing product, not just distributed infrastructure or internal tooling.

partials.job_requirement: Build and maintain features across the Linear product — from API to UI
partials.question_for_candidate: For Linear, can you walk me through a specific feature you shipped end-to-end across API and UI—what the user problem was, how you designed the backend contract, and how you implemented the frontend experience?
partials.context: What’s missing for a strong match is a concrete example of owning a product feature all the way from API design through polished UI delivery, ideally with cross-functional collaboration and clear scope similar to Linear’s product surface.

partials.job_requirement: Contribute to the public GraphQL API and developer platform
partials.question_for_candidate: At Linear, the gap is specific public GraphQL API and developer platform work—can you share a concrete example where you designed, shipped, or evolved an API/developer-facing surface and how you handled schema design, versioning, docs, or external developer experience?
partials.context: Charles shows strong REST/API and platform ownership at Microsoft, but there’s no explicit evidence of public GraphQL API or developer platform contributions, which Linear needs for this role.

partials.job_requirement: Work closely with design to ship polished, high-quality interfaces
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example of partnering with design to ship a polished UI in the Linear sense—what was the feature, how did you collaborate on the interaction/accessibility details, and what was the outcome?
partials.context: For Linear, the missing evidence is a concrete example of direct design partnership that led to a polished, high-quality interface, not just a one-time UX/UI refresh or general cross-team work.

partials.job_requirement: Help define technical direction as the team scales
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific time you helped set the technical or architecture direction for a team as Azure IoT Central scaled, and what decision you drove (e.g., service boundaries, deployment strategy, reliability, or platform standards)?
partials.context: For Linear, the missing evidence is a concrete example of Charles defining broader team-level technical direction or architecture strategy as the product or service scaled, beyond owning infrastructure and mentoring.

partials.job_requirement: React experience with component-driven UI development
partials.question_for_candidate: For Linear’s Software Engineer role, can you share a specific example where you built or refactored a React feature as reusable components, and how you structured the component hierarchy, state, and props to keep the UI maintainable?
partials.context: What’s missing is direct evidence of hands-on, component-driven React implementation rather than adjacent frontend/UI refresh work.

partials.job_requirement: Attention to product quality and design detail
partials.question_for_candidate: For Linear, can you share a specific example where you owned the polish of a feature end-to-end — from design review through implementation — and made concrete quality/detail improvements (for example in spacing, states, accessibility, or edge cases)?
partials.context: What’s missing for a strong match is a concrete example of sustained product-quality craftsmanship and design-detail ownership, beyond the one UX/UI accessibility refresh already noted.

partials.job_requirement: Experience with project management or productivity tooling
partials.question_for_candidate: Linear values product and productivity tooling — can you share a specific example where you built or improved a workflow tool (for example, Jira/Asana-like or internal planning software) and what measurable productivity gain it created?
partials.context: What’s still missing is explicit, concrete experience with project management or productivity software itself, rather than adjacent infrastructure or developer workflow tooling.

413ba9d6-57b9-457e-9ee0-dbac5b6339e5.generated_output:
Hello **Stripe**,

Given the requirements in your [Senior Software Engineer](/mock/job-mid-level-engineer) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**Five years building and operating globally distributed cloud services**

Charles brings five years of professional software engineering experience from Microsoft, including ownership of infrastructure for Azure IoT Central, a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices. His work spans TypeScript, .NET, and Go on AKS, with Docker, Helm, and Istio in production, which is the kind of backend and systems foundation Stripe’s infrastructure work demands. He also improved the service’s operational posture by overhauling CI/CD pipelines and enabling targeted microservice deployment, tripling deployment frequency. *[Resume]*

**Proven incident response and reliability ownership**

Charles was heavily involved in PROD incident response across service team areas, with explicit responsibility for hot-fixing issues and minimizing customer impact and downtime. Earlier at Azure IoT Solutions, he improved reliability and customer experience by identifying and eliminating the most common failure scenarios, and he delivered weekly incident reports to leadership across multiple teams. That combination shows he can help operate critical services under pressure and contribute meaningfully when incidents arise. *[Resume]*

**Strong backend breadth across APIs, Python, PostgreSQL, and modern frontend work**

His profile shows direct experience with RESTful APIs, Python, TypeScript, and Go, and his public project adds PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, React, and Next.js to that picture. At Microsoft, he adopted a RESTful deployment engine, built Python testing tooling for end-to-end validation, and stepped in on a UX/UI refresh when front-end engineers had moved on. That breadth is useful for a senior engineer who needs to move comfortably across service boundaries and ship production changes end to end. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**Mentorship and cross-team execution in complex environments**

Charles mentored junior engineers at Microsoft and fostered an environment where they could onboard, ask questions, and grow. He also built tooling and documentation to democratize infrastructure operations across service teams, reducing bottlenecks and making the platform easier for others to use. That combination — reducing bottlenecks at the system level while developing engineers at the individual level — is the kind of leverage a senior engineer creates, and it shows up concretely in Charles's record. *[Resume]*

---

Charles looks well suited for a senior backend role that values operational ownership, reliability, and practical systems judgment. His Microsoft experience shows he has already worked in the kind of production environment where service health, deployment discipline, and team leverage matter every day.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.job_requirement: Payments or financial services domain experience
gaps.question_for_candidate: Stripe’s backend work sits directly in payments and financial infrastructure — have you worked on any payment processing, billing, ledger, fraud/risk, or other financial services systems, and can you share one concrete example of your role and impact?
gaps.context: This matters for Stripe because the Senior Software Engineer role is building and operating critical payment systems, where domain knowledge in payments or financial services helps you make the right reliability, latency, and correctness tradeoffs.

partials.job_requirement: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Payments Infrastructure team. You will build and scale the systems that move money reliably for millions of businesses around the world. This is a high-ownership role: you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of the services you build, from design through production.
partials.question_for_candidate: Stripe’s Payments Infrastructure role needs direct experience with money-movement or payment-processing systems — can you share a specific project where you designed, built, or operated a service that moved money or handled financial transactions end to end?
partials.context: The current evidence shows strong ownership of globally distributed cloud services at Microsoft, but it doesn’t yet show a concrete example of payments infrastructure, transaction processing, or money-movement systems experience needed for Stripe.

partials.job_requirement: Design, build, and operate backend services for payment processing and financial infrastructure
partials.question_for_candidate: Stripe needs concrete payment or financial infrastructure experience — can you share a specific backend service you designed, built, and operated that handled money movement, billing, ledgering, or transaction reliability, and what scale/reliability challenges you solved?
partials.context: What’s missing for a strong match is direct evidence of building and operating payment-processing or financial-infrastructure systems, not just broadly distributed backend services.

partials.job_requirement: Improve reliability, latency, and scalability of critical payments systems
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you improved latency, reliability, or scalability for a high-traffic backend service—what was the bottleneck, what changes did you make, and what measurable impact did it have?
partials.context: The current evidence shows strong reliability and infrastructure ownership at Microsoft, but it doesn’t yet show a concrete example of optimizing latency or scalability for a payments or financial infrastructure system like Stripe’s backend services.

partials.job_requirement: Collaborate closely with product, data, and platform teams to define technical direction
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you partnered with product or data teams to make a technical tradeoff or set direction for a service, and what decision you drove as a result?
partials.context: For Stripe’s Senior Software Engineer role, the missing evidence is a concrete example of collaborating with product and/or data teams to define technical direction, not just broad cross-team or infrastructure collaboration.

partials.job_requirement: Review code, mentor engineers, and raise the engineering bar across the team
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example of a code review or engineering standard you helped enforce that measurably improved the quality, reliability, or maintainability of a team’s backend service?
partials.context: For Stripe’s Senior Software Engineer role, the missing evidence is a concrete example of formal code review and of raising engineering standards across a team, beyond mentoring and onboarding support.

partials.job_requirement: Open source contributions or public technical work
partials.question_for_candidate: Can you share a specific open-source or public technical project beyond tailord—ideally one you shipped publicly, maintained over time, or had others use or contribute to—that shows the kind of sustained public-facing work Stripe is looking for in a Senior Software Engineer?
partials.context: For Stripe, the missing evidence is sustained open-source or public technical contributions with clear scope, impact, and maintenance over time, rather than a single public repo.

Hello **Vercel**,

Given the requirements in your [Staff Engineer](/mock/job-staff-engineer) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**Cross-team infrastructure ownership that improved reliability and delivery**

Charles has owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices on AKS, using Docker, Helm, Istio, and related cloud tooling. He also overhauled CI/CD pipelines to enable targeted microservice deployment, tripled deployment frequency, and introduced region-agnostic provisioning that cut new-region setup from weeks to hours. That combination shows he can take a technical problem from operational pain to durable platform improvement. *[Resume]*

**Proactive incident leadership and systemic risk reduction**

Charles was heavily involved in PROD incident response across all service team areas, where he helped deliver hot-fix solutions that minimized customer impact and downtime. Earlier, he improved service reliability by identifying and eliminating the most common failure scenarios, and he maintained weekly incident reporting to IoT leadership across multiple teams and a third-party offering. This is strong evidence of someone who spots systemic risk early and acts before it becomes a larger incident. *[Resume]*

**Technical leadership that unblocks engineers across teams**

Charles developed tooling and documentation to democratize infrastructure operations for engineers across all service teams, explicitly removing dev-cycle bottlenecks. He also mentored junior engineers and helped create an environment where they could onboard, ask questions, and grow. That makes him a credible multiplier: not just contributing individually, but making other engineers more effective. *[Resume]*

**Strong foundation in modern web and platform tooling**

Charles’s GitHub work on Tailord shows hands-on use of Next.js, React, TypeScript, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Terraform, Azure Container Apps, AWS, and GitHub Actions in a real product. While his Microsoft experience is centered on cloud infrastructure rather than frontend product development, the repository demonstrates current fluency with the kinds of tools that sit close to Vercel’s ecosystem. That gives him a practical bridge into the company’s platform and runtime conversations. *[GitHub] [Resume]*

---

Charles brings distributed-systems ownership, incident discipline, and cross-team enablement that maps well to what Vercel's Staff Engineer role asks for technically. He is applying at a stretch level — particularly around edge compute depth, CDN design, and formal architectural authority — but the operational instincts and trajectory are clear. If Vercel is open to a conversation with someone who has the right foundation and is actively building toward Staff scope, he would welcome it.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.job_requirement: Contributions to frontend performance or Core Web Vitals at scale
gaps.question_for_candidate: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, have you led a concrete frontend performance or Core Web Vitals improvement at scale—e.g. reducing LCP/INP/CLS on a high-traffic product—and can you share the baseline, what you changed, and the measured impact?
gaps.context: This matters for Vercel because the Staff Engineer role spans platform-wide technical decisions, and frontend performance/Core Web Vitals directly affect the user experience and credibility of the edge/runtime platform.

gaps.job_requirement: Experience with serverless or stateless compute patterns (Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Lambda@Edge)
gaps.question_for_candidate: Vercel's edge runtime relies heavily on serverless and stateless compute — have you worked directly with Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Lambda@Edge, or a comparable serverless execution model? If so, share one production example including the constraints you designed for.
gaps.context: This matters because Azure Container Apps and Kubernetes are fundamentally different paradigms from serverless/edge compute. Vercel's platform is built around this model and needs Staff Engineers with direct production experience in it.

gaps.job_requirement: CDN, caching, or edge compute infrastructure design
gaps.question_for_candidate: Vercel's platform is a global CDN and edge compute network — have you designed or operated CDN infrastructure, caching layers, or edge routing at scale? Container orchestration on Kubernetes is not a substitute here.
gaps.context: This is a core infrastructure domain at Vercel. Kubernetes and Docker show strong distributed systems experience, but CDN and edge caching design is a distinct discipline not evidenced in the profile.

gaps.question_for_candidate: The Vercel Staff Engineer role requires 8+ years of experience — Charles's documented experience at Microsoft spans 5.8 years. Do you have earlier professional engineering roles, significant contract work, or pre-Microsoft experience that brings your total to 8+ years?
gaps.context: This is a hard requirement gap. The profile documents 03/2018–04/2023 at Microsoft. Without additional experience, Charles is 2+ years short of this threshold, which is a genuine barrier for this particular role.

partials.job_requirement: Mentorship and development of senior engineers
partials.question_for_candidate: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, can you share a specific example of how you mentored or developed a senior engineer—such as helping them level up technically, make stronger architectural decisions, or take on broader platform ownership?
partials.context: What’s missing is concrete evidence of mentoring or developing senior engineers, not just junior engineers, in a way that shows impact on technical judgment, ownership, or cross-team influence.

partials.job_requirement: Open source contributions or public technical writing
partials.question_for_candidate: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, can you share a specific open-source contribution or public technical write-up you’ve published that had clear external impact, and what you owned from idea to release?
partials.context: What’s missing is concrete evidence of public-facing work beyond the Tailord GitHub repo—ideally an external open-source contribution or a published technical article that shows Charles can communicate technical ideas publicly at the level Vercel’s Staff Engineer role would value.

partials.job_requirement: Vercel is the platform for frontend developers. We run one of the largest edge networks in the world, serving millions of deployments per day for teams at companies of every size. We are looking for a Staff Engineer to help define the future of our edge infrastructure and runtime environments.
partials.question_for_candidate: At Vercel, the gap is evidence of staff-level ownership of edge/runtime architecture — can you share a specific example where you defined or led a platform roadmap or architectural change that affected multiple teams or a large distributed network?
partials.context: Vercel needs proof of technical leadership beyond operating distributed infrastructure: a concrete case of setting architecture, driving cross-team decisions, and owning a platform-level initiative end to end.

partials.job_requirement: This is a technical leadership role. You will set architectural direction, drive initiatives across multiple engineering teams, and have a measurable impact on the reliability and performance of our global platform. You will be a multiplier — making the engineers around you more effective and helping Vercel stay ahead of the frontier.
partials.question_for_candidate: At Vercel, this Staff Engineer role needs someone who sets org-level architectural direction across multiple teams — can you share a specific example where you drove a cross-team technical initiative from design through rollout and show the measurable platform impact?
partials.context: What’s still missing is a concrete example of Charles formally setting architectural direction and leading a multi-team initiative end-to-end, with clear scope and measurable impact on the platform.

partials.job_requirement: Define and own the technical roadmap for edge compute and runtime infrastructure
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you defined or drove the roadmap for edge or runtime infrastructure—what problem you prioritized, how you aligned multiple teams, and what shipped as a result?
partials.context: Vercel’s Staff Engineer role needs evidence of owning a technical roadmap for edge compute and runtime infrastructure; the current evidence shows strong distributed infrastructure and Kubernetes ownership, but not a concrete example of roadmap-setting for edge/runtime at platform scope.

partials.job_requirement: Drive architectural decisions that span multiple teams and affect the entire platform
partials.question_for_candidate: Can you share a specific example at Microsoft where you drove a platform-wide architectural decision that affected multiple service teams—what was the decision, who was involved, and what changed across the platform as a result?
partials.context: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, the missing evidence is a concrete example of Charles personally leading an architectural decision with platform-wide scope, not just owning infrastructure or influencing multiple teams.

partials.job_requirement: Mentor and develop senior engineers across the organisation
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example of mentoring or developing a senior engineer or tech lead across multiple teams—what was the situation, what guidance did you provide, and what changed as a result?
partials.context: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, the missing evidence is org-wide development of senior engineers rather than junior-mentor experience.

partials.job_requirement: Proven technical leadership across multiple teams or engineering organisations
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you held explicit technical leadership authority across multiple teams — such as setting technical direction, owning a roadmap decision, or making architectural calls that multiple engineering teams deferred to you on — and what the outcome was?
partials.context: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, the current evidence shows cross-team technical influence through shared tooling and incident coordination, but what’s missing is a concrete example of formal or explicit technical leadership authority — where Charles was the named technical owner whose decisions and direction multiple teams followed.

partials.job_requirement: Represent engineering in product strategy and external technical communications
partials.question_for_candidate: For a Vercel Staff Engineer role, can you share a specific time you represented engineering in product strategy or external technical communications—such as shaping a roadmap, speaking with customers/partners, or publishing a technical update—and what impact it had?
partials.context: What’s missing for a strong match is direct evidence of owning product strategy or external-facing technical communication, beyond internal cross-team and leadership updates.

partials.job_requirement: Deep experience with Next.js or edge runtime environments
partials.question_for_candidate: For Vercel’s Staff Engineer role, can you share a specific example where you built or operated a Next.js app or edge-runtime feature in production, including the architecture choices you made and any performance, latency, or reliability tradeoffs you handled?
partials.context: The missing evidence is a concrete, production-level example of deep Next.js or edge-runtime ownership; the profile shows Next.js in a GitHub project, but not sustained implementation or edge-runtime experience relevant to Vercel’s edge compute platform.

partials.job_requirement: Ownership of engineering org-wide technical roadmap or architecture decisions
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft Azure IoT Central, can you share a specific example where you owned or influenced an org-wide technical roadmap or architecture decision that affected multiple service teams or the broader platform?
partials.context: Vercel’s Staff Engineer role needs evidence of setting technical direction at the organization level—owning a roadmap or making architecture calls that span multiple teams—beyond infrastructure ownership and tooling improvements.

partials.job_requirement: Lead cross-functional technical initiatives from inception to production
partials.question_for_candidate: At Microsoft, can you share a specific example where you were the named owner of a cross-functional technical initiative — one that you scoped, drove alignment on across multiple teams, and owned through to production — and what the measurable outcome was?
partials.context: The current evidence shows cross-team influence through shared tooling and incident response, but not formal ownership of a Staff-level initiative with clear DRI accountability from design through production.

chunks.content: Linear · Remote (US/EU) · Full-time

chunks.content: About the role
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Linear is building the next generation of project management software. We care deeply about product quality, performance, and developer experience. We're looking for a Software Engineer who sweats the details, ships fast, and wants to work on a product used by tens of thousands of engineering teams worldwide.
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has strong adjacent experience building and operating globally distributed cloud services and infrastructure tooling, including work on Azure IoT Central and customer-facing IoT platforms. However, there is no direct evidence of project management software or a product specifically used by tens of thousands of engineering teams worldwide.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has shipped and operated large-scale Microsoft services, including Azure IoT Central and Azure IoT Solutions, with a strong focus on product quality, reliability, and developer experience. While his background is not in project management software specifically, he brings the scale and engineering rigor this role calls for.

chunks.content: This is a full-stack role. You'll work across our TypeScript backend and React frontend, contribute to our public GraphQL API, and help shape how Linear evolves as a product.
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has direct experience across TypeScript backend work and React frontend work, and his GitHub profile explicitly lists React and TypeScript. He also built full-stack systems at Microsoft, which aligns with contributing to a public GraphQL API and product work.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has hands-on full-stack experience spanning TypeScript and React, including a full-stack engineering role at Microsoft and a GitHub profile that lists both React and TypeScript. He also brings experience building customer-facing cloud services, which maps well to contributing to a public GraphQL API and product evolution.

chunks.content: Responsibilities
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Build and maintain features across the Linear product — from API to UI
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has adjacent experience spanning both UI and backend-adjacent infrastructure work, including a deployment engine tied to the service UI and a UX/UI refresh, but there is no direct evidence of building end-to-end product features across an API-to-UI product surface like Linear.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has worked on both sides of the stack at Microsoft, from a deployment engine connected to the UI to a UX/UI refresh, and he also owned infrastructure for a large TypeScript/.NET/Go service. That background suggests he can contribute across product layers, even though the profile does not show a direct Linear-style API-to-UI feature ownership example.

chunks.content: Contribute to the public GraphQL API and developer platform
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has relevant platform and API experience, including RESTful APIs, FastAPI in GitHub, and infrastructure ownership for a large distributed service, but there is no explicit evidence of public GraphQL API work or developer platform contributions.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has built and operated API and platform-adjacent systems at Microsoft, including RESTful APIs and infrastructure for a globally distributed service, and his GitHub also shows FastAPI work. While his profile does not explicitly show GraphQL, he brings directly relevant backend and platform experience to a public API and developer platform role.

chunks.content: Improve application performance, reliability, and scalability
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has direct experience improving service reliability and scaling infrastructure: at Azure IoT Solutions he eliminated common failure scenarios, and at Azure IoT Central he owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices while overhauling CI/CD and enabling targeted microservice deployment.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has directly improved reliability and scalability in production systems at Microsoft, from eliminating common failure scenarios in Azure IoT Solutions to owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 40+ microservices at Azure IoT Central. His work on CI/CD and targeted microservice deployment shows he has already delivered the kind of performance and scale improvements this role calls for.

chunks.content: Work closely with design to ship polished, high-quality interfaces
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has adjacent experience shipping a UX/UI refresh and working across teams, but the profile does not explicitly show direct collaboration with design partners or polished interface delivery as a repeated responsibility.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has hands-on experience improving the user interface at Microsoft, including spearheading a UX/UI refresh with an accessibility focus. While the profile doesn’t explicitly name design partnership, his cross-team work and front-end refresh experience make him a credible fit for collaborating with design to ship polished interfaces.

chunks.content: Help define technical direction as the team scales
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has experience owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service and mentoring junior engineers, which shows influence on technical decisions as a team grew. However, there is no explicit evidence that he defined broader technical direction or set architecture strategy at the team level.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has already owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service at Microsoft and mentored junior engineers while the team scaled. That background suggests he can contribute to technical direction, even though the profile does not explicitly show him setting architecture strategy end to end.

chunks.match_rationale: Charles shows adjacent evidence of product quality and design detail through a UX/UI refresh with an accessibility focus, but the profile does not explicitly document sustained design-detail ownership or product-quality craftsmanship beyond that project.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has direct experience improving UX/UI at Microsoft, including a refresh centered on accessibility, which shows real attention to design detail. He also improved reliability and customer experience by eliminating common failure scenarios, reinforcing that he cares about product quality even if the profile doesn’t show a dedicated design role.

chunks.content: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Payments Infrastructure team. You will build and scale the systems that move money reliably for millions of businesses around the world. This is a high-ownership role: you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of the services you build, from design through production.
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has strong experience building and operating globally distributed cloud services and owned infrastructure for a large Azure IoT service, but the profile does not show direct payments infrastructure or money-movement systems experience.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has owned infrastructure for Azure IoT Central, a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices, and he has taken services through CI/CD and production operations. While his background is not specifically in payments, his experience scaling reliable cloud systems maps well to a high-ownership infrastructure role.

While his experience is not specifically in payments, he brings relevant service-ownership and infrastructure depth that transfers well to financial backend work.

chunks.content: Staff Engineer

chunks.content: Vercel · Remote · Full-time
chunks.match_rationale: Location and employment format line, not a candidate requirement.

chunks.content: About the role
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Vercel is the platform for frontend developers. We run one of the largest edge networks in the world, serving millions of deployments per day for teams at companies of every size. We are looking for a Staff Engineer to help define the future of our edge infrastructure and runtime environments.
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has direct experience owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service on AKS with Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, and Istio, which is adjacent to edge infrastructure and runtime environments. However, the profile does not show explicit ownership of edge networking, runtime platform design, or staff-level architecture for a large edge network like Vercel's.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has owned infrastructure for Azure IoT Central at Microsoft, operating a globally distributed service on AKS with Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, and Istio. That background gives him relevant platform and runtime experience, even though his profile does not explicitly show edge-network infrastructure ownership.

chunks.content: This is a technical leadership role. You will set architectural direction, drive initiatives across multiple engineering teams, and have a measurable impact on the reliability and performance of our global platform. You will be a multiplier — making the engineers around you more effective and helping Vercel stay ahead of the frontier.
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has clear technical leadership-adjacent experience: he owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service, created tooling and documentation to unblock engineers across service teams, and contributed to incident response across multiple teams. However, the profile does not explicitly show setting architectural direction at the org level or driving initiatives across multiple engineering teams as a formal leadership mandate.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has already operated as a force multiplier at Microsoft by building tooling that democratized infrastructure operations and by owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service used by 35+ engineers. He also worked across multiple teams during incident response, showing the cross-team influence this role calls for, even if the profile does not show formal architectural ownership at the highest level.

chunks.content: Strong distributed systems or infrastructure background
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has direct experience owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices on AKS/Kubernetes, using Docker, Helm, and Istio. That is strong evidence of a distributed systems and infrastructure background.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles brings hands-on distributed systems and infrastructure experience from Microsoft, where he owned infrastructure for a globally distributed Azure IoT Central service running 40+ microservices on AKS. His work with Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, and Istio shows exactly the kind of infrastructure background this role calls for.

chunks.content: Mentorship and development of senior engineers
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has direct mentoring experience with junior engineers, but the profile does not show mentorship or development of senior engineers specifically.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has already mentored junior engineers at Microsoft and helped create an environment where teammates could onboard, ask questions, and grow. That shows real coaching experience, though the profile does not specifically document mentoring senior engineers.

chunks.content: Nice to have
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Experience with serverless or stateless compute patterns (Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Lambda@Edge)
chunks.match_rationale: Azure Container Apps is a managed container hosting platform, not a serverless or stateless edge compute paradigm. The profile shows no evidence of Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Lambda@Edge, or comparable serverless/edge compute experience. These are fundamentally different execution models.

chunks.content: Contributions to frontend performance or Core Web Vitals at scale
chunks.match_rationale: The profile shows frontend work and a UX/UI refresh, but there is no evidence of performance optimization work or Core Web Vitals improvements at scale.

chunks.content: Open source contributions or public technical writing
chunks.match_rationale: Charles has public GitHub activity in the tailord repository with documentation and tooling, which is adjacent evidence for public technical writing. However, there is no explicit evidence of open source contributions beyond that repository or of published technical writing outside GitHub docs.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles shows public-facing technical output through his GitHub repository tailord, where he has documented GitHub Actions, gitleaks, and related DevOps work. While this is not a full open source contribution portfolio, it does give a recruiter concrete evidence of public technical writing.
